# json parity dashboard

Differential survey of graphviz-ts `render(g, 'json')` vs the native
`dot -Tjson` oracle over the SVG-conformant corpus set, sorted by input
size. `-Tjson` (not `-Tjson0`) is the oracle: the port structurally emits
the `doXDot=true` shape (`"_draw_": []` present on every node/edge вЂ” see
the AD note atop `test/golden/compare-json.ts`). Semantic comparison
(draw-op arrays at В±0.01, colors/fonts canonicalized, positional strings
numeric-tolerant вЂ” see `test/golden/compare-json.ts`). Regenerate:
`npx tsx test/corpus/json-walk.ts --survey && npx tsx test/corpus/json-dashboard.ts`.

## Summary

- **Oracle:** dot 15.1.0 (`-Tjson`) В· **corpus:** `~/git/graphviz/tests`
- **Walked (conformant SVG set):** 761
- **json-conformant:** 719 (94.5%)
- **diverged (tracked, will-fix):** 39 В· **accepted (documented, won't-fix):** 0
- **port-error:** 0 В· **timeout:** 3 В· **oracle-error:** 0 (excluded from scoring)

## json-conformant (719)

Port json is *conformant* with the oracle: every object/attr/draw-op
matches, numeric payloads within В±0.01 and canonicalized
colors/fonts/text equal.

_Conformant ids (719) are omitted for brevity вЂ” the full roster is in
[json-parity.json](json-parity.json)._
